title: carex norvegica var. inferalpina
intro: Carex norvegica var. infernalpina, also known as the alpine sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex norvegica plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of certain compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Carex norvegica var. infernalpina include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Carex norvegica var. infernalpina can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
